common_cold.description: A common viral infection of the nose and throat.
common_cold.care: Rest, drink plenty of fluids, and use over-the-counter cold medications. Symptoms usually resolve in 7-10 days.

influenza.description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses.
influenza.care: Rest, hydration, antiviral medications (if prescribed by a doctor). A flu vaccine is the best prevention.

covid_19.description: A contagious viral infection caused by SARS-CoV-2.
covid_19.care: Isolation, rest, hydration. Seek medical care for severe symptoms. Vaccination is highly effective for prevention.

pneumonia.description: Infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid.
pneumonia.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Treatment includes antibiotics (if bacterial), rest, and hydration. Severe cases may require hospitalization.

bronchitis.description: Inflammation of the lining of your bronchial tubes, which carry air to and from your lungs.
bronchitis.care: Rest, hydration, cough medicine. Antibiotics if bacterial. Avoid smoke and fumes.

migraine.description: A type of headache characterized by severe throbbing pain, often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light and sound.
migraine.care: Rest in a quiet, dark room. Over-the-counter pain relievers or prescription migraine medication.

tension_headache.description: A common type of headache often related to stress or muscle tension. Feels like a dull, aching pain.
tension_headache.care: Over-the-counter pain relievers, relaxation techniques, stress management.

sinusitis.description: Inflammation or swelling of the tissue lining the sinuses, often causing congestion and facial pain.
sinusitis.care: Nasal decongestants, saline nasal spray, rest, hydration. Antibiotics if bacterial.

food_poisoning.description: Illness caused by consuming contaminated food or drink.
food_poisoning.care: Hydration is key (drink plenty of fluids). Rest. Gradually reintroduce bland foods. Medical attention if severe.

viral_gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines caused by a virus. Highly contagious.
viral_gastroenteritis.care: Hydration, rest, bland diet (BRAT: Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast).

anemia.description: A condition in which there is a deficiency of red cells or of hemoglobin in the blood.
anemia.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Treatment depends on the cause (e.g., iron supplements for iron-deficiency anemia).

strep_throat.description: A bacterial infection that can make your throat feel sore and scratchy.
strep_throat.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Treated with antibiotics, pain relievers, rest, and hydration.

allergies.description: An allergic response causing cold-like signs and symptoms, such as a runny nose, itchy eyes, congestion, sneezing, and sinus pressure.
allergies.care: Antihistamines, decongestants, nasal sprays. Avoiding allergens where possible.

asthma.description: A condition in which a person's airways become inflamed, narrow, and swell, and produce extra mucus.
asthma.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed with inhalers (bronchodilators) and long-term control medications.

gerd.description: A digestive disorder where stomach acid frequently flows back into the tube connecting your mouth and stomach (esophagus).
gerd.care: Dietary changes (avoiding spicy/fatty foods), antacids, prescription medications.

ibs.description: A common disorder that affects the large intestine, causing cramping, abdominal pain, bloating, gas, diarrhea, and constipation.
ibs.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed through diet, lifestyle changes, and stress management.

uti.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, but most common in the bladder and urethra.
uti.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Treated with antibiotics and hydration.

type_2_diabetes.description: A chronic condition that affects the way the body processes blood sugar (glucose).
type_2_diabetes.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed through diet, exercise, medication, and blood sugar monitoring.

hypertension.description: A common condition in which the long-term force of the blood against your artery walls is high enough that it may eventually cause health problems.
hypertension.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed through diet (low sodium), exercise, and medication.

eczema.description: A condition that makes your skin red and itchy. It's common in children but can occur at any age.
eczema.care: Moisturizers, topical creams (corticosteroids), and avoiding triggers.

psoriasis.description: A skin condition that causes cells to build up rapidly on the surface of the skin, forming scales and red patches that are itchy and sometimes painful.
psoriasis.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed with topical creams, light therapy, and medications.

arthritis.description: The swelling and tenderness of one or more joints, causing pain and stiffness.
arthritis.care: Requires medical diagnosis. Managed with pain relievers, anti-inflammatory drugs, and physical therapy.

muscle_strain.description: An injury to a muscle or a tendon, often caused by overstretching or overuse.
muscle_strain.care: Rest, Ice, Compression, and Elevation (R.I.C.E.). Over-the-counter pain relievers.

conjunctivitis.description: Inflammation or infection of the transparent membrane (conjunctiva) that lines your eyelid and covers the white part of your eyeball.
conjunctivitis.care: Depends on the cause (viral, bacterial, or allergic). May include eye drops, compresses. Highly contagious if viral or bacterial.

dehydration.description: A condition caused by the loss of too much fluid from the body. It happens when you lose more fluids than you take in.
dehydration.care: Replenish fluids by drinking water or electrolyte solutions. Seek medical care if severe.
